title: Fix test_package_index test
body: It started to fail a few days ago, because of some extra terminal output.

step_plan.summary: Silence a non-essential debug console output in Installer.install (src/js/installer.ts) by removing the console.debug line that logs the number of dynamic libraries found, preventing extraneous terminal output from breaking tests.

steps.operation: Remove the debug console output that logs dynamic library count
steps.reason: The console.debug call inside Installer.install emits non-essential terminal output (e.g. `Found ${dynlibs.length} dynamic libraries inside ${filename}`) which can appear in test logs and cause tests (like test_package_index) that assert on precise console output to fail. Removing this single debug line preserves install behavior while eliminating the stray noise.
steps.side_effects: No change to functional behavior of Installer.install or package loading. Tests that inadvertently relied on this debug message will no longer see it (unlikely); developer debug visibility is reduced for this specific message. Should reduce spurious test failures. After change, run the test suite — if failures persist, other console.* calls may need auditing. No API or runtime contract changes expected, but downstream consumers expecting debug logs may see less verbose output.
